PLAINTIFF FRANCISCO VELASQUEZ'S MOTION TO REMOVE CASE FROM JUDICIAL ARBITRATION, MOVE TO EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM IS SCHEDULED THIS DAY. THERE BEING NO APPEARANCE NOR AN OBJECTION TENTATIVE RULING TO GRANT MOTION IS ADOPTED. MOTION IS ORDERED GRANTED. CASE IS ORDERED REMOVED FROM JUDICIAL ARBITRATION. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WITHIN FIVE DAYS. COURT ORDERED A JURY TRIAL DATE OF 6/1/2009 AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPARTMENT 206. COURT TO GIVE NOTICE OF THE TRIAL DATE AND MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY: PATRICK HEARD, CSR# 11055. (212)

ORDER RE: RULING ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EPISCOPAL COMMUNITY SERVICES OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ATERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AS TO BOTH DEFENDANTS EPISCOPAL COMMUNITY SERVICES AND CCSF. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL NOTICE OF IMPENDING ASSAULT AND BREACHED ITS MINIMALLY BURDENSOME DUTY TO ATTEMPT TO STOP THE ASSAULT. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE THAT EACH DEFENDANT SHOULD BE TREATED DIFFERENTLY GIVEN CCSF'S DOMINANT ROLE WITH ECS. ALSO, THE COURT OVERRULES ALL DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE; THE COURT SUSTAINS OBJECTIONS TO WITNESS ESPINOZA, FOR ALL PURPOSES, INCLUDING NOTICE. (PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL LEAVES PROPOSED ORDER DENYING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WITH THE COURT STAFF - WAIT FOR CRC 3.1312 - FROM TODAY'S DATE, COPY GIVEN TO OPPOSING COUNSEL AT HEARING). ORDER RE: RULING ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS FILED THIS DATE. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT EPISCOPAL COMMUNITY SERVICES OF SAN FRANCISCO, and City and County of San Francisco's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ATERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Tom Paoli, Esq. of Paoli Geerhart LLP, (415) 498-2101, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Greg C. Schaffer, Esq., of Low, Ball, & Lynch (415) 981-6630, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment is denied as to both Defendants Episcopal Community Services and CCSF. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant had actual notice of impending assault and breached its minimally burdensome duty to attempt to stop the assault. Defendants fail to demonstrate that each defendant should be treated differently given CCSF'S dominant role with ECS. Also, the Court overrules all Defendants objections to Plaintiff's Evidence; the Court sustains objections to Witness Espinoza, for all purposes, including Notice. (Plaintiff's counsel leaves proposed Order Denying Motion for Summary Judgment with the Court staff - wait for CRC 3.1312 - from today's date, copy given to opposing counsel at hearing).Order re: ruling on Plaintiff's objections to Evidence in support of opposition to Motion for Summary Judgment/or in the Alternative for Summary Adjudication is filed this date. (302CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
